title: Backend Load Remediation - Session Query Deduplication for My Pages
scope: my sessions APIs, Cosmos session reads, ownership checks
problem: Session detail endpoints currently fetch the full list of a user's sessions just to verify ownership before reading one specific session, which adds unnecessary read cost as account history grows.
risk: Users with many sessions will pay for full-history reads on every detail view, increasing latency and Cosmos cost for normal My Logs usage.

evidence.note: Specific session history reads first call get_sessions_by_user and build an owned_ids set before loading the requested session.
get_sessions_by_user returns each session's id, context, history, and _ts ordered by time, which is heavier than a simple ownership check.
The actual session history/messages are then fetched in additional read_item calls.

goals: Use O(1)-style ownership verification for single-session reads where possible.
Preserve access control while avoiding redundant full-list fetches.
Keep the sessions list endpoint functional for actual list views.

implementation_plan.action: Introduce a direct ownership helper for one session.
details: Read the target session document by partition key and compare stored user_id to the authenticated user.
Use this helper for /api/my/sessions/{session_id}/history instead of fetching the entire session list.
If the list view only needs id, context summary, and counts, avoid selecting the entire history field in the list query.
Derive history_count from a cheaper stored metadata field if available; otherwise consider adding one on write paths.

implementation_plan.action: Keep compatibility with existing response shapes.
details: Do not change frontend contracts for sessions list or session history unless necessary.
Any new helper should be internal to session_store or my_router.

acceptance_criteria: Session history access no longer requires reading the user's entire session list first.
The list endpoint avoids pulling full history blobs if they are not needed.
Ownership enforcement remains intact for private session data.
Tests cover authorized and unauthorized session detail reads.
verification: Run my_router and session_store related tests.
Add regression tests for direct ownership checks and history access denial.
Smoke-test My Logs session listing and detail retrieval.
execution_notes_for_claude_code: Favor a minimal internal refactor over a broad auth rewrite here. The main win is eliminating redundant list reads on detail fetches.
